name: Regajo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Paseo por el Embalse del Regajo
description: Enjoy an easy 4.1-mile (6.7 km) hike around the Embalse del Regajo, featuring reservoir views, greenways, and natural springs.
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails around Pantano El Regajo offer access to a diverse natural landscape in the Castellón province. The area features a natural lake surrounded by rolling hills and extensive pine forests. The confluence of the Palancia, Regajo, and Lipa rivers contributes to the region's environment. The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, Spain's longest Greenway, also passes through, providing additional routes. Best bus station hiking trails around Pantano El Regajo The most popular bus station hiking route is Jérica loop from Jérica, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the surroundings of Jérica. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castle Tower on the Hill – Medieval Castle loop from Gaibiel, an easy 3.2 miles (5.1 km) path. This route offers views of the castle tower and medieval structures, completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Local hikers also love the Medieval Castle – Bar Antonio (accommodation) loop from Gaibiel, a 11.6 miles (18.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Hiking around Pantano El Regajo is defined by a natural lake, rolling hills, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance paths. The routes in Pantano El Regajo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1,100 hikers have used komoot to explore Pantano El Regajo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many bus-accessible hiking trails are available around Pantano El Regajo?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails around Pantano El Regajo that are accessible via bus stations. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks, all starting conveniently from public transport hubs.

Q: Are there easy, family-friendly hiking options accessible by bus around Pantano El Regajo?
A: Yes, Pantano El Regajo offers several easy, family-friendly hikes that are perfect for all ages and accessible from bus stops. For instance, the region is known for gentle paths like the "Regajo Reservoir Loop" mentioned in local guides, which offers scenic views without significant elevation gain. Many of the 14 easy routes in this guide are suitable for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these bus-accessible hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails around Pantano El Regajo, provided they are kept on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other visitors. It's always a good idea to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas. Remember to bring water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see on these trails?
A: The trails around Pantano El Regajo offer diverse natural beauty. You can expect to see the beautiful natural lake, lush pine forests, and the confluence of the Palancia, Regajo, and Lipa rivers. Notable landmarks include the Regajo Dam (Presa El Regajo) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/12345678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Regajo&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint, offering panoramic views. Some routes may also pass by sections of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, Spain's longest Greenway.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes that start and end at a bus station?
A: Yes, many of the trails in this guide are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish your hike at the same bus station. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21953717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jérica loop from Jérica&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate hike starting and ending in the town of Jérica, easily reachable by bus.

Q: What is the best season to hike around Pantano El Regajo?
A: The best seasons for hiking around Pantano El Regajo are typically spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summer can be very hot, so if hiking then, it's advisable to start early in the morning. Winter offers cooler temperatures, but some paths might be damp.

Q: Are there any challenging bus-accessible hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are several more demanding routes accessible by bus. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Castle – Bar Antonio (accommodation) loop from Gaibiel&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.6 km hike with significant elevation gain, offering a rewarding experience for those looking for a longer, more strenuous adventure.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails around Pantano El Regajo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 ratings. Reviewers often praise the stunning reservoir vistas, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the well-maintained paths. The accessibility via public transport and the variety of routes for different skill levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs near the bus-accessible trailheads?
A: Yes, towns like Jérica and Gaibiel, which serve as starting points for many bus-accessible trails, offer local amenities including cafes and pubs where you can refresh before or after your hike. These establishments provide a convenient stop for a drink or a meal.

Q: Are there any routes that offer historical sights, like castles?
A: Absolutely. The region has a rich history, and some trails incorporate historical elements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle Tower on the Hill – Medieval Castle loop from Gaibiel&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that allows you to explore historical structures and enjoy scenic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What should I pack for a bus-accessible hike in this region?
A: For hiking around Pantano El Regajo, it's advisable to pack essentials like sufficient water, snacks, appropriate footwear for varied terrain, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also recommended, even on well-marked trails. If you plan to visit the reservoir, swimwear might be useful for designated swimming areas.
tips:
The Regajo Reservoir is located in the municipality of Jérica, in the Alto Palancia region, province of Castellón. This reservoir, built in 1959, is an ideal place to enjoy nature and do various outdoor activities.
To access the reservoir, you can use the CV-212 or CV-213 roads. It is a perfect place to spend a day in contact with nature.

faqs.question: Is the Walk around the Embalse del Regajo suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is considered easy and suitable for all fitness levels, including families. The paths are generally accessible, making it a great option for a relaxed outing in nature.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied but manageable. You'll walk on sections of the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, which offers flat paths, as well as through pine forests, agricultural fields, and along some roads. While mostly straightforward, some parts might involve narrow pathways or an exposed water pipe, requiring a little balance but not strenuous effort.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this walk?
faqs.answer: The walk around the Embalse del Regajo typically takes around 1 hour and 49 minutes to complete, covering a distance of approximately 6.66 kilometers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse del Regajo trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, the trail is located near Navajas and Jérica. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas in these towns or near the reservoir itself, especially if starting points are indicated for the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros sections.

faqs.question: How can I reach the Embalse del Regajo by public transport?
faqs.answer: The Embalse del Regajo is located near the towns of Navajas and Jérica in the province of Castellón. Public transport options, such as local buses or trains, might connect to these towns, from which you could then access the reservoir area. It's best to check local transport schedules for routes to Navajas or Jérica.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience the beautiful Regajo Reservoir itself, which is a central feature. Portions of the trail follow the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, offering unique experiences through old railway tunnels and over bridges. Other notable features in the wider area include the spectacular Salto de la Novia waterfall and the Fuente del Baño natural spring.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on the walk around Embalse del Regajo?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and beautiful scenery, often referred to as a 'water paradise.' The trail offers expansive views of the village of Navajas and the Regajo Reservoir. You'll pass through pine forests, agricultural fields, and enjoy lush riverside vegetation along the Palancia River. Picturesque viewpoints like the Mirador del Regajo provide excellent vistas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike around the Embalse del Regajo?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable throughout the year. Warmer months offer the added benefit of refreshing wild swimming opportunities in the area's natural springs and rivers. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures for hiking, while winter can also be mild.

faqs.question: Is the Walk around the Embalse del Regajo dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near wildlife, or on sections that might be shared with cyclists on the Vía Verde.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Walk around the Embalse del Regajo. It's an accessible public trail.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the Embalse del Regajo trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is located near the towns of Navajas and Jérica, which offer various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in these nearby villages to refresh and relax after your walk.
